title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity
description: An origin trusts arbitrary Host and X-Forwarded values, builds reset links from the request, rate-limits the proxy address, and logs raw URLs containing secrets. Learners cumulatively produce request authority and trusted-proxy decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
